title: Bhattarika Temple
tagline: Bhattarika Temple — spiritual town of Odisha

overview.short: Bhattarika Temple is located on the bank of Mahanadi River, at Sasanga village in the tehsil Baramba, (formerly Athgarh), Cuttack district, Odisha, India.
overview.description: Bhattarika Temple is located on the bank of Mahanadi River, at Sasanga village in the tehsil Baramba, (formerly Athgarh), Cuttack district, Odisha, India. It is dedicated to the Hindu Goddess Maa Bhattarika worshipped as a manifestation of Shakti. As per the Puranic tradition, Parashurama, facing certain defeat at the hands of Saharsrajuna, prayed to Durga who appeared on this spot to impart her divine power to his aid. The festival Pana Sankranti is celebrated here in April, Akshaya Tritiya in May and Dussehra in October.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Hindol State
topPlaces.description: Hindol State was one of the princely states of India during the British Raj belonging to Eastern Ganga dynasty. Its former territory is now part of Dhenkanal district.

topPlaces.name: Nilamadhav Temple
topPlaces.description: Sri Nilamadhava Temple (Odia: ନୀଳମାଧବଜିଉ ମନ୍ଦିର, କଣ୍ଟିଲୋ) is very old and famous Lord Vishnu temple which is near to the bank of Mahanadi, in Kantilo, Odisha, India. It is present near to the twin hills with a surrounding forest.

topPlaces.name: Orissa Province
topPlaces.description: Orissa Province was a province of British India created in April 1936 by the partitioning of the Bihar and Orissa Province and adding parts of Madras Presidency and Central Provinces. Its territory corresponds with the present-day state of Odisha.

topPlaces.name: Kantilo
topPlaces.description: Kantilo is a Village and Grama panchayat in Nayagarh district in the Indian state of Odisha. It is a holy place famous for "Lord Nilamadhab".

topPlaces.name: Fategarh, Odisha
topPlaces.description: Fategarh (ଫତେଗଡ) is a village and gram panchayat in Bhapur tehsil, Nayagarh district, Odisha, India. As of 2011 Census of India, Fategarh had a population of 4,611.

items.note: Hindol State was one of the princely states of India during the British Raj belonging to Eastern Ganga dynasty. Its former territory is now part of Dhenkanal district.

Sri Nilamadhava Temple (Odia: ନୀଳମାଧବଜିଉ ମନ୍ଦିର, କଣ୍ଟିଲୋ) is very old and famous Lord Vishnu temple which is near to the bank of Mahanadi, in Kantilo, Odisha, India. It is present near to the twin hills with a surrounding forest.

Orissa Province was a province of British India created in April 1936 by the partitioning of the Bihar and Orissa Province and adding parts of Madras Presidency and Central Provinces. Its territory corresponds with the present-day state of Odisha.
Kantilo is a Village and Grama panchayat in Nayagarh district in the Indian state of Odisha. It is a holy place famous for "Lord Nilamadhab".
Fategarh (ଫତେଗଡ) is a village and gram panchayat in Bhapur tehsil, Nayagarh district, Odisha, India. As of 2011 Census of India, Fategarh had a population of 4,611.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bhattarika Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bhattarika Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bhattarika Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Biju Patnaik International Airport, Bhubaneswar (59 km) and the nearest railway station is Bhubaneswar Railway Station (60 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bhattarika Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bhattarika Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bhattarika Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Bhattarika Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bhattarika Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Hindol State, Nilamadhav Temple, Orissa Province, Kantilo, Fategarh, Odisha.
